records.ety: [F. similitude, L. similitudo, from similis similar. See Similar.]

defs: The quality or state of being similar or like; resemblance; likeness; similarity; as, similitude of substance.
quotes: Let us make now man in our image, man In our similitude.
If fate some future bard shall join In sad similitude of griefs to mine.

defs: The act of likening, or that which likens, one thing to another; fanciful or imaginative comparison; a simile.
quotes: Tasso, in his similitudes, never departed from the woods; that is, all his comparisons were taken from the country.

defs: That which is like or similar; a representation, semblance, or copy; a facsimile.
